definitions: To imprison someone or to put something in a secure place.
To close a building or business by locking all the doors and windows.
To become unable to move or function, often due to a mechanical or computer problem.
To invest money in a way that makes it unavailable for a period of time.
examples: The police decided to lock up the suspect.
Don't forget to lock up the shop before you leave.
My computer tends to lock up when I run too many programs.
They had to lock up their savings in a long-term bond.
